concepts.title: Mountain Building Processes
concepts.description: There are three types of mountain building processes: Continental Collision, Oceanic-Continental Collision, and Continental Rifting. Students must be able to identify and explain each process.

concepts.title: Types of Plate Boundaries
concepts.description: The three types of plate boundaries are Divergent, Convergent, and Transform. Students must be able to identify and explain each type, including their characteristics and examples.

concepts.title: Formation of Oceanic and Continental Crust
concepts.description: The formation of oceanic and continental crust is explained by the process of sea floor spreading and continental rifting. Students must be able to describe and explain these processes.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for Plate Boundaries
concepts.description: To remember the three types of plate boundaries, use the mnemonic: 'Divergent makes new crust, Convergent pushes crust, and Transform moves it'.

concepts.title: Confusing Continental and Oceanic Crust
concepts.description: Students often confuse continental and oceanic crust. To avoid this mistake, remember that oceanic crust is denser and has a higher silica content, whereas continental crust is less dense and has a lower silica content.

concepts.title: Geological Time Scale
concepts.description: The geological time scale is divided into Eons, Eras, Periods, and Epochs. Students must be able to identify and explain each unit, including their characteristics and examples.

concepts.title: Process of Weathering
concepts.description: The process of weathering is the breakdown of rocks into smaller particles. It is of three types: Mechanical weathering, Chemical weathering, and Biological weathering.

concepts.title: Types of Deposition
concepts.description: Deposition is the process of adding new material to the Earth's surface. It is of three types: Suspension, Solution, and Settlement.
